name: MIT Recreation - Indoor Golf Range
about: The Rudovsky Indoor Golf Range at MIT Recreation is a high-tech training facility equipped with Trackman 4 technology. It offers hourly reservations for individual and group practice sessions, designed to provide comprehensive golf analysis and improvement opportunities. The venue is integrated within a larger recreational complex and offers a range of services including memberships, private lessons, and various wellness programs. While primarily focused on golf, the facility is part of a multi-sport recreation center that caters to students and community members.
why_golfers_like_it: Access to the premium Trackman 4 launch monitor for accurate data and swing analysis
Professional PGA instruction available for private and group lessons
Convenient online and mobile app reservation system for flexible booking
Located within a comprehensive fitness facility offering additional amenities

food_and_drink.notes: No on-site food service mentioned. External food allowed.

simulator_hardware.notes: Industry-standard launch monitor providing swing and ball flight data

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The facility does not specify club rental services, so it is recommended to bring your own equipment. The content does not mention any club rentals available on-site.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food or drinks?
faq.answer: There is no mention of food or drink services available within the golf facility. External food and drinks appear to be allowed, as no restrictions are listed.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The venue does not offer a traditional 18-hole golf experience. Sessions are booked by the hour for training and practice using the simulator, with no specific time allocated for a full round.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the facility offers youth golf programs and lessons, indicating it is designed to accommodate junior golfers and children.
